title: Decision on Ratification of the Operating Agreement

corpusSources.relevance: Enumerates 12 categories of matters the articles and smart contracts shall govern. The Operating Agreement supplements these per W.S. 17-31-108.
Authorizes the operating agreement to supplement matters not otherwise provided for in the articles or smart contract.
Establishes the constitutional hierarchy: smart contract > articles > operating agreement, with exceptions for 17-31-104 and 17-31-106(a)/(b).
Default fiduciary duty standard — no fiduciary duty except implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing. Operating Agreement correctly adopts this default.
Dissolution triggers. Operating Agreement Article XIII correctly enumerates all six statutory triggers plus the W.S. 17-31-105(e) identifier deadline and judicial dissolution.

analysis.reasoning: Verified that the Operating Agreement addresses all 12 categories enumerated in W.S. 17-31-106(c): member relations (Art III, XVI), member rights and duties (Art III), company activities (Art II), amendment of operating agreement (Art X), voting rights (Sec 3.4), transferability (Art XIV), withdrawal (Art XV), distributions (Art XII), amendment of articles (Art XX), smart contract update procedures (Art IX), dispute resolution (Art XVII), and all other aspects (Arts IV-VIII, XI, XIII, XVIII-XXII). Complete coverage confirmed.

Section 4.4 correctly implements the W.S. 17-31-115 hierarchy: smart contract preempts articles (except 17-31-104 and 17-31-106(a)/(b)), articles preempt operating agreement. The Operating Agreement explicitly positions itself as supplementary, consistent with W.S. 17-31-108.

The Operating Agreement creates a legally coherent framework for AI governance that satisfies both W.S. 17-31-104(e) (algorithmic management) and W.S. 17-31-114(a)(v) (natural person control). The Algorithmic Manager recommends; the Human Executor controls. The Divergence Protocol (Article VII) is a novel mechanism that preserves the Human Executor's ultimate authority while creating accountability through mandatory transparency rather than penalty.

The Operating Agreement establishes governance infrastructure that maximizes transparency, accountability, and long-term institutional resilience. The constitutional corpus system, reasoning tree publication, and on-chain hash commitments create sovereign, self-sustaining governance mechanisms. The annual review (Art XI) and inactivity safeguards compound institutional knowledge over time. The framework is designed to outlast any individual model version or API configuration, with succession planning (Sec 4.6) ensuring continuity. Strong alignment with the Maxim.

Primary risk: the legal frameworks governing algorithmically managed DAOs are novel and largely untested in judicial proceedings. The Agreement self-identifies this risk (closing paragraph). Secondary risk: Anthropic dependency (Sec 4.5) creates a single point of failure for the Algorithmic Manager role. Mitigated by succession provisions (Sec 4.6). These are inherent risks of operating at the frontier, not defects in the Agreement itself.

alternativesConsidered.alternative: Delay ratification pending legal counsel review
alternativesConsidered.rejected: The Member has explicitly chosen to self-draft without attorney representation, consistent with the DAO's ethos of direct governance. The Agreement discloses this choice. The statute does not require attorney involvement.

alternativesConsidered.alternative: Adopt a simpler operating agreement without the governance pipeline and transparency mechanisms
alternativesConsidered.rejected: A minimal agreement would satisfy the statutory minimum but would undermine the Root Thesis Maxim. The governance pipeline, reasoning tree publication, and transparency obligations ARE the Company's value proposition. Simplification would reduce the Company to a standard LLC with no meaningful algorithmic governance.

reasoningTree.traceabilityChain: Ratifying the Operating Agreement establishes the institutional infrastructure for sovereign, self-sustaining governance. Every provision — the Algorithmic Manager framework, the Divergence Protocol, the Constitutional Corpus, the transparency obligations — serves the Maxim's directive to compound human agency over generational timescales. The on-chain hash commitment ensures institutional memory persists beyond any individual session, model version, or human lifetime.
